title: Kuzhanthai Velappar temple
tagline: Kuzhanthai Velappar temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kuzhanthai Velappar Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Poombarai near Kodaikanal in Dindigul.
overview.description: Kuzhanthai Velappar Temple is a Hindu temple in the village of Poombarai near Kodaikanal in Dindigul. Around 10 to 12 centuries after returning from China, Bogar completed the Palani Andavar statue. He built one more Navabasanam Statue at the midpoint of the Palani and Poombari Western gates. Nowadays, its location is referred to as the Yanai Gejam (Bogar Forest), as per inscriptions in the temple which temple has built by king of the Chera dynasty.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Poombarai
topPlaces.description: Poombarai is a village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Sheep Research Center (Mannavanur)
topPlaces.description: Southern Regional Research Centre (SRRC) is a regional centre of the Central Sheep & Wool Research Institute (CSWRI), a premier Institution of the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (I.C.A.R), New Delhi. The SRRC was founded on 16 November 1965 and has been active for over 45 years at Mannavanur.

topPlaces.name: Guna Caves
topPlaces.description: Guna Caves, initially named as Devil's Kitchen, is a cave located in Kodaikanal, Tamil Nadu, India. It attracts many visitors every year.

topPlaces.name: Berijam Lake
topPlaces.description: Berijam Lake is a reservoir near Kodaikanal town in Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, South India. It is at the old site of "Fort Hamilton", in the upper Palani hills.

topPlaces.name: Mannavanur
topPlaces.description: Mannavanur (Mannavanor, Mannavannur), is a farming village of 1437 households in Mannavanor Panchayat, Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, India, 35 kilometres (22 mi) west from Kodaikanal It is 7 kilometres (4 mi) by road from Kumbur village and 9 kilometres (6 mi) from Kilanavayal village. Altitude is 1,880 metres (6,170 ft).

topPlaces.name: Kodaikanal block
topPlaces.description: Kodaikanal block is a revenue block in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 15 panchayat villages.

topPlaces.name: Kodaikanal division
topPlaces.description: Kodaikanal division is a revenue division in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India.

topPlaces.name: Kodaikanal taluk
topPlaces.description: Kodaikanal taluk is a taluk of Dindigul district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Kodaikanal.

items.note: Poombarai is a village in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

Southern Regional Research Centre (SRRC) is a regional centre of the Central Sheep & Wool Research Institute (CSWRI), a premier Institution of the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (I.C.A.R), New Delhi. The SRRC was founded on 16 November 1965 and has been active for over 45 years at Mannavanur.
Guna Caves, initially named as Devil's Kitchen, is a cave located in Kodaikanal, Tamil Nadu, India. It attracts many visitors every year.
Berijam Lake is a reservoir near Kodaikanal town in Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, South India. It is at the old site of "Fort Hamilton", in the upper Palani hills.

Mannavanur (Mannavanor, Mannavannur), is a farming village of 1437 households in Mannavanor Panchayat, Dindigul district, Tamil Nadu, India, 35 kilometres (22 mi) west from Kodaikanal It is 7 kilometres (4 mi) by road from Kumbur village and 9 kilometres (6 mi) from Kilanavayal village. Altitude is 1,880 metres (6,170 ft).
Kodaikanal block is a revenue block in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 15 panchayat villages.
Kodaikanal division is a revenue division in the Dindigul district of Tamil Nadu, India.
Kodaikanal taluk is a taluk of Dindigul district of the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The headquarters of the taluk is the town of Kodaikanal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kuzhanthai Velappar temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kuzhanthai Velappar temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kuzhanthai Velappar temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (89 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (87 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kuzhanthai Velappar temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kuzhanthai Velappar temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kuzhanthai Velappar temple?
faq.a: Stays in Kuzhanthai Velappar temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kuzhanthai Velappar temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Poombarai, Sheep Research Center (Mannavanur), Guna Caves, Berijam Lake, Mannavanur.
